penalties.description: Task was not completed - not all objects were successfully transferred in both directions as required by FLS Peg Transfer protocol.

consensus_score.reasoning: Both models agreed on completion time (133 seconds) and that the task was not completed successfully. While Claude identified this as a different exercise entirely, GPT-4o recognized it as an incomplete Peg Transfer attempt. The consensus applies the incomplete_task penalty resulting in automatic zero score, as the FLS protocol requires all six objects to be transferred bidirectionally for successful completion.

claude_value.description: This appears to be a needle transfer training exercise, not the standard FLS Peg Transfer task with six objects requiring bidirectional transfer.

disagreements.rationale: Both models agreed the task was not completed successfully. Whether it was a different exercise or incomplete attempt, the FLS rubric requires automatic zero for incomplete tasks.
GPT-4o provided more specific phase identification that aligns with observable activity, while Claude's assessment was overly broad.
FLS rubric requires automatic zero for incomplete tasks. GPT-4o's score calculation ignored the incomplete task condition.
